title: Knitted Cats & Kittens

review.text:
KNITTED CATS & KITTENS has patterns for some reasonably cute knitted toys, but most don't look much like cats or kittens to me. My favorites are: "Fluffy Cat" (a fairly realistic kitten, knit in white angora yarn); "Floppy Moggy" (a fairly realistic "longhair" kitten, knit in black fur yarn); "Doorstop Cat" (a brown knitted doorstop); "Monster Cat" (a turquoise alien cat with wide-open red mouth); and "Cosy Toes" (a cat-shaped hot water bottle cover, knit in fur wool yarn).

A cute twist is the inclusion of patterns for "accessories" for the cat toys: a scratching post, a cat bed, balls for batting around, and tiny mice. There is also a kitten in a Christmas stocking with a mouse on the toe, to be used as a tree decoration. As knitted toy patterns go, these patterns don't look particularly difficult. However, they do require knitting a lot of small, shaped pieces that must be seamed up, stuffed, and sewn together.

Because I don't like any of the designs well enough to want to knit them, I rate KNITTED CATS & KITTENS at 3 stars ("It's okay" on the official Amazon scale). The "Look Inside This Book" feature shows all of the included designs as a gallery in the Table of Contents. Cats seem to be difficult to translate into knitted toys, but if you're looking for patterns for knitted cats, you might want to consider [[ASIN:1579128939 Knit Your Own Cat: Easy-to-Follow Patterns for 16 Frisky Felines]], a book of patterns for small "pedigreed" cats to display.
adorable- you can make a whole litter!
This book has a wide variety of kitties to make. Many patterns can be adapted to personalize the kitty so it looks more like your own feline friends
Lots of cute projects.
The book is fantastic for all knitting cat lovers.  Great fun and easy patterns
Fabulous!

As a lover of cats, I was intrigued to find at our library yet another knitting book on little animals. Usually cats get only a small mentioning in a book like that. But this one features LOTS of little feline cuties!

I like that these cats aren't meant to look 'like the real thing', but rather a bit well, amusing. We have a long hair cat (a Ragamuffin) and like that this book features a way to make cats with eyelash yarn. What a neat idea. There are so many different kitties here, not just the same old thing over and over. The cat with the knit flowers and embroidered leaves and vines on her back is lovely. That way your kitty can be coordinated with your room's colors. What fun.

As for practicality, I like that each row and object you are making (i.e. arms, front, back) is clearly marked, no searching around for where you are constantly. Each finished size is stated in the first section of the pattern too. You do need to know how to knit when using this book; no time is spent in teaching those skills. And not a ton of time is spent in teaching you how to embroider, but enough of the basics are here. Also, keep in mind that these patterns tend to call for many different pieces that will need to be sewed together. The finished kitty ranges in size from 2 - 16 inches, with most in between.

It's funny, the doorstop is what our kitten will eventually look like, fat and happy. That's the first one on my list to knit. :-)
Fun projects with easy to follow instructions.
My mom has really enjoyed this book.  And I got a gift of a Monster Cat from her!  Purrrfect! Creative book!
Adorable book of adorable cat to knit. Can't wait to get started. My grandkids and mother-in-law will love these when finished.

I have recently begun knitting and crocheting infant and toddler items for a local cooperative for moms with small kids. These will be a great hit, I am sure. I have made two little cats so far. They are adorable! They work up quickly and make perfect toys for very young kids. If you like to knit and are tired of doing hats and scarves, these will enchant you and whoever receives them!

I lent it to a knitting friend and she is so enthralled I haven't seen the book for 2 months!! SHe is
enjoying it too! I think Ill have to buy another and just gift the other to my friend. Charming book

Its a cute book but there are problems with how the patterns are written. she doesn't write her patterns as row one, row two and it is very difficult to follow them. The cats are knitted flat and then sewn together, which is great if you do not know how to use dpn's but I find making stuffed toys on dpn's much easier and for some of the smaller cats it is much easier to make the legs and tails on them. I am currently working on the grey cat with the flowers on it and its coming out pretty well, and I have made the Siamese cats. all the patterns I have done have been completely marked up with row numbers to make them easier to follow.  Also it should be noted that she starts a lot of her patterns in the info paragraphs before the work starts. The cats are very cute and work making, but you will need to read the pattern and make lots of notes to be able to work them. I do not recommend this book for beginning knitters at all, you will also need a fairly good basis in sewing and how to shape things.

This is a very cute book making adorable toys. I disagree with some other reviews in that I think these look plenty like cats and kittens.
I gave this book 3 stars only because of the numerous messed up instructions. I have found other reviews on other sites as well as the ones here talking about how you need to fudge and make up your own instructions where what is provided just isn't clear. Right now I am working on Alley Cat. Instructions are provided giving stitch count and it jumps from 24 to 14 with only a two stitch decrease. A couple rows later it gives a 26 stitch count where the instructions don't add up. I couldn't find any published revisions anywhere. I'm not a beginner knitter but I'm not really experienced either. This is a disappointment as I want to enjoy my knitting not be frustrated by it.

Bought this as a Christmas gift for my sister.  She loves knitting and she loves cats so I thought it was a win-win.  She indicated she really liked it, and she's not one to go out of her way to mention something specific like that out of all the gifts I got her for Christmas if she doesn't mean it.  I can't say if you or your friend or family member will like this book, but if they are a knitter and like cats, or just want something fun to knit, I'd be willing to bet they'd at least get a kick out of it.
Bought as a gift.  She loves the book.
Very clearly written and adorable patterns!
Saw this for my sister and she loves it. has made many kitties from it. The Monster Cat was a present for my grandson and he will love it. So many cute kitties!

I loved this book until I started to knit up the patterns. I wanted to make several of the patterns but they are NOT user friendly. I am an advanced knitter and hand knit pattern drafter (USA); and really found some of the shaping incomprehensible and others with errors in printing, and some just plain no fun. She overdoes the shaping decreasing and increasing which I admit makes them look so good but not much fun at the tight tension she uses. She knits with heavier yarn at a tight tension which I find hard on my aging hands.

This is not for beginners. I ended up last night thinking that I would draw patterns and sew these cats with fabric. Also she is attached to the M1 increase which I find slow and unfun although elegant. So although I love the pictures in the book I have yet to complete a cat, but today I will once again confront the problems with ALLEY CAT. I think I will end up making my own patterns and corrections. So be aware that this is an adorable book with significant problems and needed pattern testing by normal people before publication. Sigh...what a disappointment.

I received this lovely book in great condition, and within a reasonable time from my ordering it. I have not attempted making any of the items yet, but have many requests, and cannot wait to begin. I have knitted dogs, but not yet cats.

I will return to let anyone that is interested, how I found the patterns.
This book has a variety of cat/kitten patterns.  They are all cute and the directions seem well written.
Fun Patterns!  Made a bunch of the mice.
Book in perfect condition.  Quite a variety of kitty cat pattern choices with large pictures and clear instructions.  I recommend it.
Love this book!
This book has some really cute cats to knit. I plan to get yarn on payday to knit 3 or 4. My niece's son has put in a request also.  :)
Found the yarn to make Floppy Moggy. The patterns look really well done and it's a darling book.

I bought this book to make a cat for my young niece who loves cats.  The patterns are not realistic, but some still embody enough "cat" for me and  I chose the Alley Cat for its sly, comical take.  LUCKILY, I checked on Ravelry to see other people's results before starting to knit.  I discovered errata there and then looked around online and found an even longer errata page from the publisher.  Hoping I had avoided the frustration of pattern errors, I embarked on my cat and quickly became frustrated by the unnecessarily obscure directions which don't use consecutive numbering for rows, but repeatedly use "Row1" "Row2" which makes it difficult to keep track of where you are in the pattern.  Also no gauge is offered.  The direction to sew arms and legs into the body seams has already been mentioned by others as awkward.  And finally I discovered yet another error in the directions for which I had to compensate.  This book, or at least this pattern, could have used a much more thorough editing job.  I will not be using this pattern or any other in the book again.

So,I'm in the middle of trying to knit "Floppy Moggy" one of the patterns in Knitted Cats and Kittens. The knitting techniques called for with this cat are not terribly difficult; however be aware that some guess work and slight alterations must be made to what is shown in the book. I'm not going to go into great detail about these changes as any capable knitter would see what I mean. One example is that with this particular pattern there is a part which says "Knit from * to * to shape the front legs." (page 80) Unfortunately, there is no first *, only the second * so I had to guess where the first * should be. Thankfully, it was fairly obvious where the first asterisk should have been. I hope there won't be very many similar problems with other patterns in the book. There are other patterns I plan to try because there are some very cute cats! Overall, the cats are cuter, to me anyway, than the patterns in "Knit Your Own Cat" (I have that book also) some of which look quite strange. Anyway, it is a sweet book, just be ready to get creative with some of the directions.

Let me start by saying that this is a wonderful book! The kitty patterns are so cute, and the design and layout are fun, playful and witty. It's enjoyable to look at and read, and for that alone I would give it 5 stars.

But I had to knock a star off because it would have been great if, when creating these patterns, the author had thought about how to minimize the sewing and seaming. There's only one pattern knit in the round on DPNs, and I think there should definitely have been more. Also, when knitting flat, were there opportunities to pick up stitches on the body to add the arms and legs? I'll be checking with my more experienced knitter friend (for whom I also bought a copy of this book), but it would have been nice if the author had included a broader variation of patterns from simpler to more fiddly.

I also agree that the instructions are not written for beginner knitters; there's a lot of shorthand and gaps that you are expected to figure out (e.g. the body of the one DPN pattern has instructions to repeat the last 3 rounds once more, then says to 'work 6 rounds.' Work in what?? A more experienced knitter who understands the mechanics of knitting in the round will know this means to knit 6 rounds, but a newbie might be tripped up to knit the previous rounds which would add inadvertent increases.)

Another reviewer commented that these cats don't look 'real'; I agree but in my opinion that's a plus. These are meant to be cute toys, and that they are. (I also own 'Knit Your Own Cat', and I think the patterns in that book are slightly weird looking.)

Nonetheless, though I wish there were more DPN patterns and some that were less fiddly, I think this is an adorable book and would definitely recommend for all cat-loving knitters!
Fun book for people who enjoy knitting like me!

title: A Vintage Affair

review.text:
I have a hard time coming up with a genre for this one, reviews have said it's a lovely romantic comedy and she is described as writing chick-lit. But first there is no humor in this one, and yes it has romance but not any more than a normal book. It's not focused on the romance., and i don't want to call it chick-lit. Let's just call it a "normal" book with romantic tendencies. But the book is so much more than that, it's about friendship and loss, and there is a story about WWII that almost breaks my heart and I keep on hoping.

Phoebe has lost her best friend and she is trying to cope with that, she has left her boyfriend, and she blames herself, him, and she missed Emma. How she die you learn after awhile as Phoebe talks with the old lady telling her story about Emma, their friendship and how it all ended. She is trying to go on and she has bought a store and transformed it into a vintage store since that is the clothes she love. Then one day she gets a call from an old French woman who wants to sell her clothes, but in her closet there is a little blue coat and Phoebe gets to hear a story as she visits, a story about friendship during a hard time in occupied France. There are similarities between their stories and perhaps they can find forgiveness and friendship in each other.

I enjoyed this book a lot and I could not put it down. Isabel Wolf weaves a wonderful and delightful story and the corner stones are the vintage clothes that Phoebe sells. They make the story. I feel like I want to wear them too, even though I am like Phoebe's mum, I don't want to wear old clothes. But they seem so lovely, and unique. She explains about the clothes, their history and meaning. Even if I don't know everything she makes me know.

There are sad parts too, I had hope for so long time and kept turning the page and wanting. To learn the truth, and I did feel sad at times. It does deal with some heavy things.

And yes there is some romance, she meets a man, is he the man, or is someone else for her. But I was always more fascinated in Phoebes relationship with the old woman, and the two stories belonging to that. But the romance needed to be had so she could move on and love again, because I wanted her to be happy. There is also the story about her parents and the search for understanding and forgiveness there, so there are some themes that echo in the book.

Delightful and wonderful is how I would like to describe it. It makes me want to go out and find a vintage shop. A very sweet read that made me hold my breath at times. I want to read more by this author.
this book came to me in great condition.  it also arrived very quickly.  i am very pleased with it all.  :o)

title: I Escaped The World's Deadliest Shark Attack

review.text: Very exciting book! My daughter loved it! It's an interesting story, especially since it's true!
Gift for 9 y o grandson who really enjoys these survival stories ies

My 8 yr old son read it on his own. He liked it but some parts were a bit gross and scary for him. It is along the lines of the I Survived books. He said it was a good story because it was based on real life even though some of the parts were a little scary.
Pretty cheap book. Looks like it was written and published in someone’s garage.
My son loves these books.
Another great series of your child enjoy the I survived books

Josh became a WW2 sailor to avenge his father's death at Pearl Harbour. But when a Japanese torpedo strikes the USS Indianapolis, he is left to fight for his life with hundreds of other seamen while they battle hunger, blazing sunshine, no fresh water and swarms of sharks.

This fictional account of a true WWII disaster is an action-packed, tense read from cover to cover. The horrific situation is brought to life through the eyes of a sixteen-year-old boy who questions his place in the war, his responsibilities at home and his difficult feelings about the cultural conflict. The story never lets up as Josh faces one terrifying situation after another and keeps readers engaged with an array of realistic characters and honest conversations -- both internal and external -- about complex topics.

I especially enjoyed the extensive back matter including a factual section about sharks, World War 2 facts tailored for young readers and information about the controversial handling of the USS Indianapolis wreck.
My kids loved the book and I also enjoyed it very much. I just don't believe a children's book should have foul language. It was really not necessary.

This was a great read about the USS Indianapolis and a 16-year-old sailor onboard. This story concisely explains what happened to the USS Indianapolis after the ship was bombed by the Japanese. Mistakes were made on the part of the Navy and American lives were lost so this story isn’t well known in history, I guess it was buried. This book does a good job of telling what happened and detailing the mistakes that were made that cost lives. A great adventure tale of survival.  Great follow up story about a 12-year-old who set the story right and shows how young people can be real heroes too.
Perfect for summer time reading ! Any kind of reading actually! My Grandson could not put the book down .

title: Caravan: A Novel

review.text: One of my favorite books of all time. Dorothy gilman is a master of words.
One of my favorite Dorothy Gilman stories. When she does a non-Mrs. Pollifax story she is a really fabulous storyteller. I was glad to replace the copy someone borrowed and never returned.

I have always liked Dorothy Gilman's Mrs Polifax novels. They are well-written spy stories with danger and suspense, but no graphic violence or immorality.

In Caravan, Gilman writes a much more serious story of survival and hardship. Without giving the plot away, i will say that the story is riveting and well written. What kills the story is the sexual immorality she includes-not just mentioning it, but JUSTIFYING it! No matter what hardships we face, we MUST stay true to right values and live a decent life-this the main character (Charissa) failed to do.

When she found out that her husband killed her lover, she contemplating killing him, but didn't. Good-it showed she has decency and a knowledge of right and wrong. But this evaluation was turned on its head when instead she enters an adulterous relationship for DECADES! It is really showing how sick society is when this is supposed to be the heroine of the story!

This book provides a lot of insight into the lifestyle of those who live in the desert.  If you like Dorothy Gilman's other books, you'll find this one more intriguing than most.  UNlike most of Ms. Gilman's books, this one is definitely NOT humourous.
It kept me wanting to know more.

Recently I was introduced to the writings of Dorothy Gilmore. Yes, I enjoyed the Mrs. Polifax series, but Caravan shows the depth and expertise of Gilmore in storytelling. Her descriptions of the desert are magnificent and I felt like I was there. The love story added to making this book even more interesting with a surprise twist at the end. Please, do not read the ending first because it will spoil the delightful trip through Gilmore's mind and eyes. I highly recommend this book because it is not only well written, but it is also a page-turner. Bettye Johnson, award winning author, Secrets of the Magdalene Scrolls.

Caravan is an incredible book. For 11 years I had a medically fragile daughter and kept Caravan in a packed emergency bag for dashes to the Childrens Hospital 2 hours away. On many occasions, I would be at the hospital for weeks and would always re-read Caravan. The strength of the main character, her ability to "roll with the punches" was such an encouragement to me. Sections of the book spoke to me when I needed them the most. At one point Jared is near death and we are quoted "Our doubts are traitors and make us lose the good we oft might win by fearing to attempt...". My child was near death and risking the scorn of the ER doc, I called up the medical director of the hospital to demand a neurosurgeon consult. Whew, did that get action! My child had emergency surgery and lived to see another year. Dorothy Gilmans Pollifax books are great but none can touch the depth of Caravan. Thank you Dorothy!

title: Land of Careful Shadows (A Jimmy Vega Mystery)

review.text:
I just finished reading Land of Careful Shadows (proof copy). It was great! For starters it was written like a pro, the story line was complicated enough, characters were ones the readers could "get" and a nice cross section of people too. It was just a darned good read - that will be passed along.

I just finished reading Land of Careful Shadows It was great! For starters it was written like a pro, the story line was complicated enough, characters were ones the readers could "get" and a nice cross section of people too. It was just a darned good read . The part that really touched me was seeing our friends from south of the border (legal and illegal) - seeing their view of life, their customs etc. Like nearly everyone, I've had contact with hispanics and sensed the cultural divide, but the characters in the book told their story. I'm sure this additional insight will be helpful in future interaction with them. Alas! there is a divide.

Despite my age,, I found Jimmie Vega quite attractive. Lucky Adele. The cops I've known are more like old Greco.

I understand Ms. Chazin is working on a sequel. I'm waiting - - - -

Often a well-written "literary" mystery sacrifices suspense. Not this one. I kept turning pages to solve multiple puzzles --chiefly who killed an Hispanic woman whose body is found without ID in a northern NYC suburban reservoir. But I also enjoyed Chazin's characters (esp Adele who reminded me of Supreme Court Justice Sonia Sotomayor) her apt descriptions of anti-immigrant discrimination (subtle and overt) and the romance.

I thought that this was a really well-done detective story. This is one of those books that I have had no my tbr forever but kept skipping over it for one reason or another. I am glad that I finally dusted it off and gave it a try. I liked this book from that very start and found that I felt invested in the mystery. I had a great time with this book.

Jimmy Vega is a detective in New York state. He is called to the scene when the body of an undocumented female is found. Jimmy is a Latino and he realizes very quickly that the small town that he is working in may not give equal treatment to everyone. There is a large immigrant population in Lake Holly with many being undocumented. Not only does Jimmy want to learn how the woman died, but he is also trying to find out what happened to the child in the photograph that she had with her.

I liked Jimmy right away. He was a smart cop and because of his heritage, he was able to see both sides of the case a little clearer than some of his co-workers. He was determined to get answers about the woman's death and make sure the child was safe. He also wasn't afraid to question how things have been done in town and worked to right as many wrongs as he could. He had a few more personal matters to deal with in this story which made him seem a little easier to relate to. He doesn't do everything perfectly in this story but he works to learn from his mistakes and I always felt like he was trying to do what was right.

I loved the way the author was able to incorporate the lives of undocumented workers in this story. I found that the way she painted the picture of what their lives were like both in this country and at home was really powerful. I thought that the motivation of the characters to risk so much to come to the states for a chance to work felt very authentic. I really liked the way that we really were able to see both sides of this story.

Armando Durán was the perfect narrator for this story. There was quite a bit of Spanish sprinkled throughout the book and I thought that he made everything flow very well. Don't worry, you don't need to know how to speak Spanish to enjoy this book (I don't) but I thought the fact that the narrator made those passages sound natural was a big plus. I thought that he handled all of the character voices very well and was able to add a lot of excitement to the story. I wouldn't hesitate to listen to his work again.

I would recommend this book to fans of detective stories. I thought that this was a nicely plotted, complex mystery with really fantastic characters. I would not hesitate to read more of the Jimmy Vega series!

I received a digital review copy of this book from Kensington Books via NetGalley and borrowed a copy of the audiobook from my local library.

I selected Land of Careful Shadows because I have enjoyed reading Suzanne Chazin's other books.  This book was interesting and well written. I read it in two days. Once Chazin set the stage and I got to know her characters, I couldn't put the book down. The book has lots of twists and turns and rich characters.  I didn't realize how complicated life becomes for the undocumented.  I understand much research went into the accuracy of their lives ... I commend her for telling their story. I'd highly recommend the book to anyone who likes good writing, rich characters and an interesting plot.  I didn't want the book to end, hoping a follow-up is in the works.
This is a murder mystery with many twists and turns.  It describes the problems that undocumented people have trying to work and live in America.  The murder has a real surprise twist.

Thank you for sharing what undocumented members of our communities experience every day through an engaging mystery. I worked with many of the folks Chazin describes in an adult literacy program and while I understood some of what they'd experienced clearly there was much I didn't know or understand.  I wish I'd had Chazin's book back then!  I enjoyed the mystery and the twists and getting to know the characters. I feel like we're just getting started with them and look forward to more!

I just finished reading LAND OF CAREFUL SHADOWS by the brilliant, relatively new, author  Suzanne Chazin​. I have now found another superb author to follow. Her characters are as real as in person flesh and blood. Her writing if fluid and descriptive without being flowery. The plot is rich and instantly engaging Suzanne Chazin​ is added to my favorite author's list.
What an amazing book! I couldn't put it down. I've been a fan of Suzanne Chazin's since her days at Reader's Digest.
Suzanne Chazin brings you right into the story and then keeps you there. I was sorry when it was over!

Chazin’s characters attract and sustain attention. Their reactions to life’s challenges consistently enhance their distinct and colorful personalities. There are no apparent bad guys to incite simplistic judgment so that even criminal suspects delight with multilayered humanity.
Usually I tire of mystery series books, but am hopelessly intrigued with Puerto Rican detective Jimmy Vega including his personal and professional life. What will he and Adele Figueroa face next? I can't wait for the next title.

By the end of the first few chapters, Chazin's ability to write convincing Latino characters caused me to research her background and what ethnic influences support her talent. Her openness and sincere desire to know and learn from undocumented immigrants to the New York area gained their trust and freed them to reveal the risks and dangers every present as they struggle to support their families back home with “courage and dignity.” Their gift to her is credibility supporting Chazin’s portrayal of Hispanic cultures while respecting anonymity through fiction.

Land of Careful Shadows is an engrossing page-turner without one dimensional fluff. Instead, Chazin peppers the narrative with sharp, clean and delightfully innovative descriptions that seamlessly propel the action forward to the gratifying surprise ending.

This is a mystery with great relevance to the problems of undocumented migrants.  Written with a great deal of empathy for them, it's obvious that Chazin has done a good deal of research on the plight of migrant workers.  The characters are very believable and fully developed.  .  It kept me guessing to the end.  I highly recommend it.

What a great book for our mystery book club! I really loved the characters especially Greco.  The subject of illegal immigration is so appropriate for the times we are living in. Our book club had a heated discussion on the topic. This book gave everyone a human perspective on illegal immigration.  I loved the mystery-didn't see the end coming.  Good book!

title: Blood Infernal: The Order of the Sanguines Series (Order of the Sanguines Series, 3)

review.text: Read the whole series!! Very good storyline.. Kept you on your toes through all 3 Books!!

Very dark and incredible imaginative writing once again by the master, James Rollins. Great trilogy in this series.

Recommended? Absolutely.
James Rollins is an awesome writer His books are the best I have read
Rollins is the best, storytelling is his soul.Read  Blood Gospel first, then the other two.
This was a GREAT series, one I will read again. Well put together--the premise sounds silly, but the details made it come together very well. Good suspense, good writing, good story!

Wow, I was out of breath when I finished this book! Rollins and Cantrell are a great writing team - he of the nonstop action thrillers, she of the mystery novels.

In this satisfying conclusion to the Order of the Sanguines trilogy, Jordan, Erin, and Rhun continue on their mission that was started in "The Blood Gospel." (I should pause here to say that this series of books MUST be read in order, or you won't understand anything that's going on.) Along with the usual cast of characters - Cardinal Bernard, Elizabeth of Bathory, Father Christian, Sister Sophia - the trio find themselves on the run in a mad dash to save the world while eluding some very nasty beasts. Elizabeth plays a very large role in this book, and I liked her portrayal. Suffice it to say that she's not entirely the same as how she is portrayed in the previous two books, and it actually works nicely.

Overall, I really enjoyed this book. It was well-written, with a gripping plot, lots of action, and excellent character development. Nice going, team Rollins-Cantrell; I hope you'll collaborate again in the future.

I really enjoy Rollins. His books always offer fast-paced action, and, being based on rather unique interpretations of historical events or legends, tend to fascinate me. This may not be writing at its highest form, but it is very entertaining, and is a wonderful way to spend an evening. However, you may find yourself pulling an all nighter, so plan accordingly.
GREAT READ

I thoroughly enjoyed the first two novels in this trilogy---at least that was what I thought it was going to be. Now I see it listed as "The Order of the Sanguines Series" and this third book ends in such a way that future books are certainly possible. I think that disappointed me the most.
The writing is tight. I have read other books by these authors which they wrote independent of each other. They both write a good story.
"Blood Infernal" will likely not make much sense if you have not read the first two books in sequence.
I enjoy the fact that historically based detail is correct but the authors have added complicated twists that keep the suspense and intrigue taut. There is a strong spiritual connection in this series. It is not just a good vs. evil plot. I appreciate the strong reminder that choosing the "right thing" for others is not always easy nor the best way to proceed.
Great story love Rollins
You need to read all three books to get the whole story.  Somewhat dark in places but good read.
Just absolutely marvelous. I’m fascinated how everything unfolded and ended on the note of my preference. Such a wonderful reading that I will marvel for awhile.
The last book in the Sanguine series.  Excellent read.
Wife bought this and she doesn't do reviews.
All his books are great-REAL PAGE TURNERS!!!!
James Rollins books are excellent, great story teller.
I was ecstatic to discover this sequel to a memorable story line that leaves you hoping for more. Great job.
The book continues the level of excellence that the first two established.  Highly recommended.

I have read all three novels in this series, and my impression, not just of this book, but the series itself is that this is a solid entry in the mystery/thriller/conspiracy genre.

Characters: the only one of the main three I did not particularly care for was Jordan Stone. He felt a little too one-note, too "upright do-gooder" and therefore, was not a fully-fledged character in my mind. He was too white knight I suppose, especially when contrasted with Ruhn Korza and his dark past. I don't have a special love for anti-heroes, but of the two men, Ruhn came alive more on the written page as an actual person, and not a stock character. This is surprising because I distinctly remember feeling [negatively] surprised and disappointed in the first novel when we find out Ruhn is a vampire [or as this series calls them, strigoi]. I suppose Rollins/Cantrell felt they too had to jump on the vampire train like so many others in recent years. I just recall feeling tired of the whole vampire craze, but I must say, Ruhn, and the strigoi as a whole, didn't bother me as the series went along. As for the last main character, I could take it or leave it. I've encountered others similar to Erin Granger in other media and for me, I understand the role she plays in propelling the plot, but I am neutral on Erin overall.

Plot: This book wrapped up the series in a tightly paced, focused manner. It was a near-page turner for me. I liked the inclusion of real life historical people and places and the storytelling flowed rather well - making for easy reading.

Conclusion: This is where the book doesn't quite succeed. I'm glad that all three characters get a "happily ever after" because that's a feel good ending. However, realistically, at least two of the three should have faced much more tribulation [and even death] than they did. Indeed, characters would suffer so-called mortal blows, but through various plot devices, none of the main characters faced permanent death. This greatly lessens the suspense and fear we readers would feel for the characters when we know ahead of time, the authors will devise an "out". Still, I would recommend this book/series with a marginal thumbs up.
The final book in this series filled in all the blanks that was missing with a little twist faith.

Had a rough time reading the novel. I was utterly confused, and had a tough time finishing the novel. Strigoi, sanguinists, Lazarus, and lucifer are the main focus, and I had a tough time believing the overall plot. I realize that this is fiction, but the book tested my patience.

I loved Rollins SIGMA series.  However, this series really affects me as twisting the Words of God, which I have a very hard time with as a Christian.  And please, all you non-believers, don't write to me about my faith.  I found all three of these books very difficult to get through and was glad to have an end to it.  They are perverse and of the dark side, but again, this is just my opinion.  If Rollins continues in this vein, I will not bother buying anymore of his books.  I love love love SIGMA and hope it will return, because the research and truth throughout those books was really amazing.

I was so looking forward to this book and at the same time sad to see this story finally end. This is one of the coolest vampire series that I have ever read. I love how they put historical characters in the story. This book starts out with a bang grabs you by the throat and doesn't ever let up til the very end.
They have done a fantastic job finishing the story. I loved every bit of it. Will warn you though there are some very sad parts. All in all though a great ending to a fantastic trilogy. Anyone who has read the previous two and has not read this one, you need to get off your butt and read it. Quit putting it off. It's amazing.
Janes did it again.
good book

I had bought the first book in the trilogy, "Blood Gospel". For some reason this book sat on my bookshelf unread for several years. I was thinning out my bookshelf to donate books I have read and others I had no desire to read for whatever reason, to my local library. "Blood Gospel" was in the pile to be given away. However, something prompted me to read the dust jacket, hmmm, seems interesting, then I started reading the first few pages... then I was hooked. This is a great story, it turned out to be one of those books I could not put down. "Blood Infernal" (#2 in the trilogy), was the same. After reading the first installment (Blood Gospel), I had to have the second and third! I was completely pleased with all three books and yearned for more... Those two writers were excellent collaborators.

Rebecca and James have outdone themselves in the wrapping-up of this series... It is a sad day, since I've finished the book-let's hope the world is at peril once again so the trio in this trio can come back. Not that I'm wishing for natural disaster or anything here, but..... (My faith is in the trio)

Not only does this book perfectly fall inline with the other two in the series, it falls inline with something worldly (at the time of its publishing.) Only the VERY most clever will figure it out. I, did not. I'm leaving it at that because I never divulge spoilers. I want you to enjoy that 'AH-HA' moment as I have. But I will tell you it is so cleverly plotted that I am green with envy at its brilliance. Can you tell I'm having a hard time not spilling it???

A guaranteed thrill ride...
I am almost done and I will hace completed the trilogy. An excellent read. I highly recommend.
This was a really great 3rd and final installment in this series.  The ending was a wee bit - odd...but other than that, an excellent adventure!
As always....James Rollins delivers with another great novel.  Wish this wasn't the last in the series, but I take it-it is?
Third book of the trilogy, tied up all loose ends and was a good and exciting read!
Awesome book

I have read almost every book that James Rollins has written, so it's safe to say I'm a fan and that it's very likely I'll read his next book. Having said something nice, I have to say that Blood Infernal is disappointing. I won't spoil it for those who haven't yet read it, but even if you're not a Christian (which I happen to be), I think you'd find the ending implausible. Coleridge wrote that in order to truly appreciate poetry, the reader must have a willing suspension of disbelief. In order to appreciate the ending of this novel, the reader who is a believer has to willing suspend his or her beliefs. It has bothered me from the beginning that the premise of the whole series is that Lazarus was not miraculously raised from the dead by Christ but that he was a vampire who sustained himself on blessed wine (the "blood" of Christ). I could tolerate that, though, as a bit of poetic license. I can't tolerate the notion that Eve's "sin" in the garden was not following through on a promise to the serpent, Satan. Eve disobeyed God. Satan, Lucifer, whatever you want to call him, fell before humans were created, and Adam and Eve had nothing to do with his fall. I hope the next book is not such a disappointment.

I just finished reading Blood Infernal, the third and final volume of Jim's and Rebecca's The Order of Sanguines series. The quest to find Lucifer and the 'Angelchild' has brought our four characters to the ultimate battle between good and evil.
I have been seeing posts of so many people who 'steam-rolled' their way through this book in a day. I couldn't. I'm too reluctant to say goodbye. I have been on the most exhilarating, exhausting, frightening and satisfying roller coaster of a ride through this trilogy of books. I have laughed, cried, screamed and covered my head with my sheets in fright while walking alongside these characters. The closer I got to the end of this third volume, the slower I would read. I couldn't let go. I tried to limit my reading to the evenings after I retired, but found myself stealing precious work-allotted minutes during the day. Dr. Erin Granger, Sergeant Jordan Stone, Father Rhun Korza and Countess Elisabeta Bathory have walked out of my life headed to new, unknown encounters where I can't follow.
In the first volume, Blood Gospel, we are introduced to Dr. Granger, Sergeant Stone and Father Korza who are thrown together investigating the discovery of ancient ruins uncovered after an earthquake at Masada, Israel. There, in a hidden tomb, they learn about a book written in Christ's blood by His own hand - The Blood Gospel. Their quest is to find this book.
In the second volume, Innocent Blood, we are thrust back into the dark world of the Sanguines, an immortal group founded on the blood of Christ. Subsequently, they must find and protect a young boy who is believed to be an angel given flesh. Their search takes them to all corners of the earth where they wind up locked in a battle at the gates of Hell.
This has been the most gut wrenching goodbye (to four people who I have come to consider as close friends) I have ever had to experience. Jim and Rebecca set out writing this series hoping to pull us into a world of the unknown, residing in their imagination, where we had never been. Wow, did they ever succeed! I don't want to leave. Kudos, you fantastical, Wizards of Words. Even though I read as slow as I dared, it still came to an end. I envy Jim's and Rebecca's ability to follow this group as they cross the threshold in that hidden Sanctuary far beneath St. Peter's Basilica. But, you never know. With a little hope and a whole lot of luck, one day, we may turn the corner in some dark, back alley on our literary journeys to stumble upon four old friends who have been just as anxious to visit with us again, as we have been with them. Thank you, Rebecca. Thank you, Jim. Please, continue to wield your mighty pens to conjure up and write about more magical destinations and characters for us to discover and fall in love with all over again.

Love the Sanguine series. It's different. And keeps you on the edge of your seat. The characters are fun to follow, even though some are scary. If you don't like Sci-Fi or didn't enjoy James Rollins other books, this isn't for you. I have been a fan of James Rollins for a long time and have read nearly all of his books. I wasn't sure if I'd be happy with Cantrell's writing, but she brings a new style to Rollins' storytelling.
What a great writing pair.
Fascinating story line if you like to read about vampires.

As I read the reviews I saw "I couldn't put it down" over and over. I have to join in on that refrain. Blood Infernal is a great conclusion to a great trilogy. It was packed with action, suspense and just enough romance and romantic tension to give it that extra edge without being sappy. The Order of the Sanguines is the most interesting and imaginative take on the vampire genre I've ever read.
Sorry for the series to end, great story and keep your interest throughout.

This is the third book in the Order of the Sanguines Series. The first was Blood Gospel, then Innocent Blood and last this one. Also there were two short stories that pertained to this series on Kindle (City of Screams and Blood Brothers). I was totally surprised by this series. James Rollins' books always contain so much historical data, it's like taking a history lesson from your favorite professor. There were a lot of Bible verses in this book too. You are not only kept on the edge of your seat trying to second guess what is about to happen, but the pace is so fast. My comment to a fellow reader was "You are not going to believe how this book ends!" When reading Rollins' books, I always find myself reading faster and faster to try to finally know how the characters' lives are going to turn out.
I loved the way this series ended! Super book and series!

Talk about saving the best for last!  The end of the trilogy is by far the best of the three.  Too bad the story has to end.  The twist in the last three pages seems to leave the door open for another book, however.

Great series. Fast read. James Rollins has quickly become an elite author in my collection.
Book arrived on time in great shape.
A gift for my husband.
Excellent read
I love this series!
This is a great book!
Interesting book. Good.
As expected
Not as good as the first and second books of this series.
Outstanding fiction.
I love James Rollins books.  This series is very interesting and keeps me on the edge of my seat.  You should check it out.
The thrilling end to a great trilogy!
I love James Rollins's writing. I've read all his books and when I told a friend that it had vampire priests she had to read them too. I prefer the Sigma series but this one is great, too.
Book was excellent & arrived on time & didn't even look like it had been read before!
Enjoyable series.  Quite happy with the story, and characters.
James Rollins keeps you glued to the book. If you like mystery and action he is your man.

This book was INTERESTING.  It was this author's style of action that keeps bringing me back to reading his books.  I have collected all of his books and keep them in order to read them again which I have and will continue. The story line and the action is GREAT!!!!
Another Fantastic read from James Rollins and Rebecca Cantrell!
Haven"t finished yet, But is culminating up to be an excellent wrap of the Trilogy.
Such fast-paced action, I found myself speed reading to keep up with all the intrigue.  Great trilogy.  Hope some of the characters make a reappearance in one of Mr. Rollin's future novels.

I was fortunate to receive this book as a give away from William Morrow Publishers and was not disappointed!

Being the last book in this series, I knew there were going to be many loved characters eliminated from the story...that always grips my heart since they have been so prominent in the books. Jim & Rebecca can weave a tale that is so suspenseful & full of terror that I grip the book until my knuckles are white. The Trio - Warrior of Man, Woman of Knowledge & Knight of Christ are such a magnificent team, always saving the world from demons, strigoi, but this time from Legion & Lucifer himself. It is a roller coaster thrill ride taking you to different parts of the world and delving into the Catholic church & prophecies.

To understand Blood Infernal, please read the series in order - The Blood Gospel, Innocent Blood, then Blood Infernal. Truly the best thriller series to date!

Thank you Jim and Rebecca for creating these wonderful & memorable characters...I hope you collaborate more in the future.
The seller delivered as promised & the book is a great way to conclude the trilogy.
nicely done

I love James Rollins and I've read all his books. Teaming with Rebecca Cantrell took this to another dimension, that I found rather pleasant and exciting. I've read a number of her books and they are very realistic and graphic, but combined with the adventures of a Rollins novel it works without being overpowered by the horror and grittiness.
A Fantastic ending to a GREAT trilogy! It has surprises in it up until the last paragraph! My favorite series, to date!

This is the 3rd and final book in the Order of the Sanguines trilogy. I read the first 2 books and eagerly awaited the 3rd. There's always great expectation for the final part of a story and that book always has a lot to live up to. In this case I found it to be OK. Disappointing because James Rollins hasn't disappointed before and also because I enjoyed the first 2 books. Rhun was my favourite character throughout and I didn't particularly like how his story developed and ended here. Erin I found annoying at times and I really never warmed to Jordan. As I said before the book was OK but did not live up to expectations. This series must be read in sequence to be understood.

This is the 3rd and final book in the Order of the Sanguines trilogy. I read the first 2 books and eagerly awaited the 3rd. As I said before the book was OK but did not live up to expectations. This series must be read in sequence to be understood.
it was the finale of a trilogy

I have really been enjoying this series -- the characters, the dialog, the action and suspense and the overall plot. The only problem I have had while reading it is the cognitive dissonance that results from reading a story ostensibly set in modern times but that seems to accept a very literal interpretation of biblical history. Everything that happens in the books only makes sense if you accept that the entire universe consists of the Earth and a bunch of shiny lights in the night sky, that the Earth was created 6000 years ago, that Noah was a historical figure, that Lucifer fought against the Archangel Michael right here on Earth, and that Hell is some place deep underground. I cringed when I read that the reason why the dark essences of strigoi defeated in battle wafted downward was because they were returning to Hell whence they came.

Even if you are religiously-minded, it's hard to reconcile such a medieval world view with the rest of the story that involves modern technology and attitudes. It just takes me out of what is otherwise a very interesting and well-written story.

In the end, I managed to convince myself that the story takes place, not in our modern world, but in some alternate universe where the Earth was created 6000 years ago and really the center of a very small universe, where God didn't have to worry about billions and billions of light years worth of other galaxies, where Satan really was banished underground, and where the Bible really is a historical record of actual events. I'm not saying you have to be a Young-Earth Creationist to enjoy this book, but it does help to imagine that this novel takes place in a universe where the Young-Earth Creationists are actually correct and all those pretty lights in the sky really are just pin holes in the fabric of the night sky.
KEPT MY ATTENTION ALL THE WAY THROUGH.

I really like this series by Rollins and Cantrell and I am disappointed that it will end with this book.

title: Gone Tomorrow: A Jack Reacher Novel

review.text: Child brings another tale of Jack Reacher's travels in an itinerant and mobile lifestyle. Reacher is one of the most human characters brought to the mystery/thriller page in some time, a contrast sharpened by his prowess, size, and strength. a captivating story that warrants rereading.
Jack Reacher always ends up kicking butt and taking names.  This one really twisted around unexpectedly, I really expected the bureaucrat to be the bad guy.
There is no one who writes better spy fiction than Lee Child.

Prior to this, I read 14 Child/Reacher novels. 12 were excellent. 2 were below his usual high standard. Had this been my first Child/Reacher novel, it would have been my last. What a load of garbage was published under Lee Child's name.  Lee, please explain to your fans why you permitted such dribble to be given your name as it is obvious that you did not write this worthless "novel."
excellent service, thank you!

In "Gone Tomorrow" Lee Child comes up with yet another twist on putting Jack Reacher in the middle of a lose-lose situation and somehow, he crafts a believable way out for our favorite drifter. After reading "Die Trying" I instantly became hooked on the Jack Reacher series. You would expect that after a dozen books in the series Child would run out of plausable situations for Reacher to fall into and the plot line would become predictable....well you would think wrong. I look forward to reading "Worth Dying For" and "The Affair" Child's newest in the series. Now if they could have only gotten Mike Rowe from the Ford car commercials to play reacher instead of Tom Cruise.....
great

"Gone Tomorrow"

This book proved to be one of the best Jack Reachers yet. There were no holds barred and a cozy it ain't, not by a long shot.
Fasten your seat belts and prepare to be held fast by THE Jack Reacher.

I can't say that the ending alone held me spellbound. It was so much more than just the ending. (If I can say JUST the ending) If any of you have read some of Lee child's Jack Reacher books than you already know that Jack doesn't get cornered...he gets even and then some. This book goes beyond that and we can never get enough. Jack's enemies become our enemies. We know that it's Jack's side we are on and we (the reader) hold fast to it.

I won't bother going into the details of this story since over 450 other reviewers have done that ...just to say get Jack in "Gone Tomorrow". It's a must read!
Please take note of Dick Hill reading on CD and doing a splendid job. Jack's total assurance of a win-win situation in the face of death comes through in Dick Hill's reading.

This one's hard to review because any little detail I reveal might turn out to spoil a surprise for someone. I will say this was an exciting, satisfying read. Much more like his earlier Reacher books that got us hooked. Once you get past a certain point in the book, there's no good stopping point. You just have to keep reading right on through. Don't expect perfect plausibility. It's a thriller, so let yourself go and enjoy. There are even a few good laughs along the way. The thing about the rubber gardening clogs had me cackling.

Jack Reacher witnesses a late-night tragedy involving a woman on a New York subway. He is approached by various people who know he was a witness. They ask him questions that make no sense to him, and he decides to figure out why the woman did what she did on the subway. Stubborn and arrogant as ever, Reacher refuses to back off regardless of the danger level. It's an elaborate cat and mouse game through the streets and subways of New York City. Who's the cat and who's the mouse remains to be decided as the story progresses. There's only one small thing left unrevealed at the end that I really wanted to know. We can only hope it will be revealed in a future installment.

This book was very long and boring.  It took forever to get any type of action other than our hero walking around New York, pretending to be a bum, beating up some guys and then doing the same thing over and over.  What a lame scenario. The story and action started in chapter 77.  This was my first Reacher read and I hope the rest are so much better than this.
This is the first JR book that I have read, but it won't be the last. I did not expect much, as this is not my usual genre. But it grabbed me from the first chapter and never slowed down.
Lee Child has done it again.  If you like any of the Reacher novels, then this is the next book on your reading list.
Great reading couldn't put it down...  finished it in four days!
very satisfied

I am more than halfway through reading all the Lee Child Reacher novels. They are a smooth and easy flowing read. You like Jack Reacher and through the books, he has some predictable characteristics and yet some unpredictable moves. I figure out some things, but not all the twists, which come as a nice surprise and solid endings.
Suspense, and a good guy kicking ass! The whole series is awesome.
Ordered by my husband who has read most of Lee Child's books. He enjoyed this book and has passed it on so others can enjoy it too.
Reacher is always good, however this one was really reaching. How far does this MP have to walk to get a job done. Whew!
As usual, Lee Child crafts a typical Jack Reacher  story. Well crafted

I've read just about all the Reacher books.  This is my all-time favorite.  It grabs you from the very beginning and, as the cliche says, it doesn't let go.  When I try to lure a friend or relative into trying out Reacher, this is the book I give them.  And there are a number of Reacher books I didn't much care for at all (take that, Blue Moon!).  But this is a keeper.

For fans of Jack Reacher, as I am, this might seem not up to pare with all the other books that Lee Childs has written. It was a little slow in spots and at times, it was hard to follow the story but in the end, it made me want to finish it and drew me in.
Good read

Child is one of my favorite mystery writers and Jack Reacher is one of my favorite good guys. There is just no bull from this ex-MP. This is #14 in the series and it is no slacker. True, there are some Reacher novels that I have liked better, however, I may be getting confused. Please allow me to explain.
Of course, the movie version just recently aired starring Tom Cruise as Reacher. This is like Billy Crystal playing John Wayne. It just don’t work. Jack Reacher is 6’4” and can defeat and disarm many bad guys in a variety of ways. He is also very resourceful, and only carries what he needs – which would be a toothbrush, basically. There is no overkill on weapons or ammo. He buys cheap clothes at K-Mart and such places. He wears them a few days and throws them away – just like Tom Cruise would not.
This book begins with a potential subway bombing in NYC and escalates from this point. It is a very good book. It is not my favorite of the series, but it is very good. It has all of the elements that we have come to expect from our Jack Reacher novels and this one does spin into a good tale. I hate to give too much away and this is one of those that one should just read. I would recommend this book to any Lee Child fan, fully aware that they would not be disappointed.
I have not read this one yet , But  the whole series are great stories and I totally enjoy them.
Great book!
i am tired of the formula

them and none have disappointed.
Enjoyed!

This was the first Lee Child book I've read, and I'm not sure there will be another. It started off with a bang, but as the book progressed the story line for me became more and more unbelievable. Suddenly I'm looking up and wondering how we got to the point where the characters were shooting gurellia tranquilizer into each other. And how Reacher figured out the case at the end was totally unbelievable. So all in all, it was just ok with me. Hopefully there are better Reacher novels out there.
Not very good

i love this writer!  no one beats Jack Reacher...Lee Child can't write these books fast enough for me!  read this series.

suspenseful

I have bought about ten Reacher novels so far and am slowly completing my collection of the whole series. With this one, however, my experience was totally negative. Almost as soon as I started reading the book, the pages started falling out like autumn leaves. By the time I got to the end, I was picking them up from the floor and trying to sort them into the proper order. In the end I gave up and still don't have the patience to find out what happened. Just was was in the photo which would damage the Afghans?
So, if the publisher reads this, a substitute copy would be very gratefully received! It might also give me more confidence in buying the remaining books in the series.
Love all these books
Lee Child never disappoints
For a change this particular Jack series did not grasp me as quick as the previous but by 3/4 time it had me gripped once again.
all jack reachers somewhat predictable but very good

This story started out interesting enough, with a suicide of a young woman, on a train, but soon sunk to an aimless search for the reasons for this suicide, with one boring, endless & action-less goose chase after another. Just when think think there will be some sort of conflict, Jack Reacher always seems to get out of the situation without swinging a fist, shooting someone, or just making the story interesting for the reader. This is my first Jack Reacher novel, and judging from the lackluster way this novel was written, probably my last. I say probably, because every novelist occasionally comes out with  a boring novel, and for the author's sake, I hope this wasn't his best effort. A novel without conflict is boring, and the only conflict I experienced while reading this story was to ask myself, why I was bothering with it. Needless to say, I didn't bother finishing it.
Wow!  Jack Reacher is the best super detective.  Sees people being wronged and does something about it, within his set of ethics.

Every one of Lee Child's books regarding Jack Reacher is outstanding and a tremendous joy to read.  They are intriguing, captivating, and you never want the stories to end.  I never want to put one of his novels down once I start reading.  You get the feeling that you want the stories about Jack Reacher to go on and on and on, because he is just great and interesting.
The opening subway car sequence is a masterpiece of sustained menace and tension. Another superb novel from Lee Child.
Love the Reacher books!
Excellent reading.
